name: uTuring Machine
description: Compact 6HP port of the Music Thing Turing Machine Mk II. Random looping sequencer on a 16-bit shift register — big probability knob locks or mutates a clocked CV loop. Backpack header drives Volts, Pulse and Voltages expanders.

controls.name: Big knob (probability)
controls.description: Sets probability of flipping the first bit each clock. Noon is fully random; extremes lock the loop; small offsets slip occasional notes.
controls.detail: noon: random · 3 o'clock / 9 o'clock: slips occasional notes · fully CW: locked loop · fully CCW: locked loop at 2x length

controls.name: Length
controls.description: Selects the loop length in steps. Combines with the big knob at fully-CCW to double the effective loop length.
controls.detail: 2 · 3 · 4 · 5 · 6 · 8 · 12 · 16 steps · doubled when big knob at fully CCW

controls.name: Write
controls.description: Momentary 3-way switch. Up injects a 1 into the first bit each clock (fills the register); down injects a 0 (clears). Centre is normal.
controls.detail: up: force 1 · centre: normal · down: force 0 · acts on clock edge while held

inputs.name: CLOCK IN
inputs.description: Advances the shift register on each rising edge. Square waves, triggers and audio-rate clocks all work; audio-rate produces metallic noise-like textures.

inputs.name: KNOB CV
inputs.description: CV input summed with the big knob position. Modulate with LFO or envelope to jump between locked and random states without touching the panel.

outputs.name: CV OUT
outputs.description: Main stepped DAC output. Sum of the last 8 bits of the shift register; 256 discrete levels.

outputs.name: PULSE OUT
outputs.description: Gate output tapped from the register — goes high on clock steps whose bit is 1. Rhythmic accent trigger that tracks the locked melody.

behaviors.name: Shift-register mechanics
behaviors.description: Each clock shifts bits right and re-fills bit 0 with a copy of bit 16 XORed with noise, mix set by the big knob. Extremes yield pure feedback (locked loop) or pure noise (random).

behaviors.name: Loop locking
behaviors.trigger: big knob fully CW or CCW
behaviors.description: Fully CW locks the pattern into a loop of the length set by the switch. Fully CCW locks into a 2x-length pattern. Small nudges away from extremes sprinkle single-note mutations.

behaviors.name: Write-switch editing
behaviors.description: Edit a locked loop live. Up gradually fills the register with 1s (rising melody); down with 0s (dropping). Tap on specific beats to carve phrases out of a random stream.

behaviors.name: Expander bus
behaviors.description: 16-bit parallel bus exposes every register step. Tunefish's own Volts (5 pots summed to one CV), Pulse (multiple gate outs) and Voltages (8 faders, dual CV) expanders all tap this bus — same protocol as the Music Thing Modular originals.

behaviors.name: Audio-rate clocking
behaviors.description: Feeding an oscillator into CLOCK turns the module into a chaotic digital noise source. CV OUT becomes metallic grit tracked by the VCO pitch; PULSE OUT becomes a rhythmic subharmonic.

patchIdeas.name: Locked generative melody
patchIdeas.patch: External clock drives a locked 8-step loop, CV OUT goes through a quantizer to an oscillator, and nudging the big knob CCW introduces slow mutations.
steps: Set the LENGTH switch to 8.
Turn the BIG KNOB fully clockwise so the loop is locked.
Patch your external clock (e.g. Pamela's) → uTuring Machine CLOCK IN.
Patch uTuring Machine CV OUT → quantizer IN; set the quantizer to minor pentatonic.
Patch quantizer OUT → VCO V/OCT.
Patch uTuring Machine PULSE OUT → envelope TRIG; envelope → VCA CV; VCO → VCA → output.
To evolve the phrase, nudge the BIG KNOB just CCW of fully CW — notes will swap out one at a time.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CV OUT — stepped 0 to ~5 V pitch CV into a quantizer, then to a VCO's V/OCT.
patchIdeas.hear: An 8-note in-key melody that repeats exactly while locked, with PULSE OUT punching envelopes on the high notes. Nudge the big knob CCW and single notes begin to swap, mutating the phrase.
patchIdeas.diagram:
uTuring Machine:
* Length: 8 · Big knob: fully CW (locked)

Clock:

Quantizer:

- Clock (Out) c> uTuring Machine (CLOCK IN)
- uTuring Machine (CV OUT) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- uTuring Machine (PULSE OUT) g> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Probability modulation
patchIdeas.patch: A very slow LFO into KNOB CV drifts the module between fully random and fully locked without touching the panel.
steps: Set the BIG KNOB to noon (fully random).
Set the LENGTH switch to 8.
Patch a slow LFO (triangle, ~0.05 Hz / 20-second cycle) → uTuring Machine KNOB CV.
Patch your external clock → uTuring Machine CLOCK IN.
Patch uTuring Machine CV OUT → VCO V/OCT (optionally through a quantizer).
Patch uTuring Machine PULSE OUT → envelope TRIG for rhythmic accents.
Listen for the LFO peaks — the probability virtually sweeps to the extremes, locking the loop for a few bars before unlocking again.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CV OUT — stepped pitch CV that cycles between random and locked melody, feeding the VCO.
patchIdeas.hear: The module breathes between chaos and order: random burbling when the LFO is near zero, and a settled repeating melody when the LFO peaks. Great for unattended live drift.
patchIdeas.diagram:
uTuring Machine:
* Big knob: noon

LFO:
* Rate: 0.05 Hz triangle

Clock:

VCO:

- LFO (Out) >> uTuring Machine (KNOB CV)
- Clock (Out) c> uTuring Machine (CLOCK IN)
- uTuring Machine (CV OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Write-switch loop sculpting
patchIdeas.patch: Lock the loop CW, then hold the WRITE switch DOWN on strong beats to carve phrases out by forcing zeros into the register.
steps: Set the LENGTH switch to 16.
Turn the BIG KNOB fully clockwise to lock the loop.
Patch your clock → uTuring Machine CLOCK IN.
Patch uTuring Machine CV OUT → VCO V/OCT (optionally via a quantizer).
Patch uTuring Machine PULSE OUT → drum module TRIG.
Let the loop play until you hear a phrase you like.
Hold the WRITE switch DOWN on strong beats to inject 0s — the drum thins out as register bits clear.
Flick WRITE UP on other beats to inject 1s and rebuild density. Release to centre to re-lock whatever pattern you land on.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CV OUT — locked stepped pitch CV, editable in real time; PULSE OUT — gate that thins/densifies as you edit.
patchIdeas.hear: A locked 16-note line you sculpt live: WRITE DOWN drops notes out of the melody and sparsens the drum, WRITE UP fills it back in. Performance-ready subtractive editing.
patchIdeas.diagram:
uTuring Machine:
* Length: 16 · Big knob: locked CW

- Clock (Out) c> uTuring Machine (CLOCK IN)
- uTuring Machine (CV OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- uTuring Machine (PULSE OUT) g> Drum (Trig) // thins out as register clears

patchIdeas.name: Pulse expander groove
patchIdeas.patch: Add the Tunefish Pulse expander · route several derived gate outputs to kick, snare and percussion voices · keep the core loop locked for repeatability while the pulse subset creates polyrhythms.
steps: Connect the Pulse expander to the uTuring Machine backpack header (power off first).
Set the LENGTH switch to 16.
Turn the BIG KNOB fully clockwise to lock the loop.
Patch your clock → uTuring Machine CLOCK IN.
Patch Pulse OUT 1 → Kick TRIG.
Patch Pulse OUT 2 → Snare TRIG.
Patch Pulse OUT 4 → HiHat TRIG.
If the pattern is dull, nudge the BIG KNOB CCW one click to swap a bit and re-lock — repeat until the groove lands.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Pulse (Out 1/2/4) — three 0/+5 V gate streams into three drum trigger inputs.
patchIdeas.hear: A locked 16-step drum pattern where each pulse output fires on its own subset of steps — kick, snare and hats sit in different-but-related rhythms like a hand-drawn euclidean groove.
patchIdeas.diagram:
uTuring Machine:
* Length: 16 · Big knob: CW locked

Pulse:
* gate outs from register bits

Kick:
Snare:
HiHat:

- Clock (Out) c> uTuring Machine (CLOCK IN)
- Pulse (Out 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Pulse (Out 2) t> Snare (Trig)
- Pulse (Out 4) t> HiHat (Trig)
